Doctor.title: Doctor
Doctor.description: Represents a doctor in the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Doctor entity.

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the doctor is available for appointments today.

Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient in the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Patient entity.

medicalHistoryIds.description: References to MedicalHistory entries. (Relationship: Patient 1:N MedicalHistory)

MedicalHistory.title: MedicalHistory
MedicalHistory.description: Represents a patient's medical history record.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicalHistory entity.

prescriptionImageURL.description: URL to the prescription image uploaded by the patient.

Hospital.title: Hospital
Hospital.description: Represents a hospital in the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Hospital entity.

AppointmentToken.title: AppointmentToken
AppointmentToken.description: Represents an appointment token for a patient.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AppointmentToken entity.

tokenNumber.description: Token number assigned to the appointment.

definition.description: Stores hospital information. Each document represents a hospital.

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: The unique identifier for the hospital.

definition.description: Stores doctor information. Requires the hospitalId to be set to determine the hospital relationship.

params.name: doctorId
params.description: The unique identifier for the doctor.

definition.description: Stores patient information. Each document represents a patient.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the patient.

definition.description: Stores the medical history of a patient.  This is a subcollection of the patient document to easily retrieve a patient's history.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the patient.

params.name: medicalHistoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the medical history entry.

definition.description: Stores information about appointment tokens. Each document represents an appointment token.

params.name: appointmentTokenId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment token.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, following the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization of authorization data. Specifically, any subcollection that depends on its parent's data for authorization copies relevant fields (e.g., hospitalId in doctors) into the subcollection's documents, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. Structural Segregation divides data with differing access needs into separate collections (e.g., separating doctors and patients data). Access Modeling employs consistent patterns: path-based ownership for private user data (e.g., `/patients/{patientId}/medicalHistory/{medicalHistoryId}`) and membership maps for collaborative access (not explicitly needed, but a possibility if Doctors and Patients were to have a many to many relationship). QAPs are supported by segregating collections based on access control requirements. The structure uses explicit state modeling (`status` field) and predictable schemas with semantic naming for data clarity and consistency.
